demographics.living_situation: resident in a nursing home / residential aged-care facility

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Call placed by the facility registered nurse with the resident present.) The resident is saying: "My mouth and throat are on fire, and I can't even swallow my own spit. It hurts too much to drink." The nurse adds: she hasn't been able to eat, drink or take her tablets for about half a day now and I'm worried she's getting dehydrated.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It has got much worse over the last day — she can't swallow anything at all now, not even her saliva, and she's had nothing to drink for about 12 hours, so we didn't want to wait.

symptom_narrative.onset: sore mouth started about 3 days ago and has got rapidly worse over the last 24 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly 3 days, with the last 12 hours being the worst — unable to swallow at all
symptom_narrative.character: an intense burning, on-fire feeling on the tongue, cheeks and throat, with thick white stuff coating the inside of the mouth
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: she says it is the worst pain, so bad she cannot swallow her own saliva and keeps spitting it out
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the tongue, the inside of the cheeks, the roof of the mouth and down into the throat
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting steadily worse; every attempt to swallow is very painful
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing seems to help; she is too scared of the pain to try to drink
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: swallowing anything — food, fluids or even saliva
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: she feels weak and drowsy and has stayed in bed; she has not managed her usual morning tablets
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bed-bound today because of weakness; completely unable to maintain her fluids or take her medications

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: high blood pressure that she takes daily tablets for
recently treated for a urine infection with a course of antibiotics that finished a few days ago

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: daily blood-pressure tablets (currently not given because she cannot swallow)
just finished a 2-week course of antibiotics for a urine infection

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged-care facility; frail and normally needs help with daily activities; nursing staff are present and monitoring her

objective_data_offered.value: thick white curd-like coating and patches over the inside of her cheeks, the roof of her mouth and the back of her throat, seen on the video call
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: seen on a nursing-home video call; lighting and angle limit the view and no proper mouth or throat examination is possible remotely

objective_data_offered.value: her lips and the inside of her mouth look very dry and cracked
she keeps spitting her saliva into a bowl because it hurts too much to swallow it, and the full jug of water by her bed has not been touched
she has had no food, fluids or her usual tablets for about 12 hours and seems weak, drowsy and more tired than her normal self
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: nurse's observation over video; no measured vital signs available on this call
